large-v3-turbo.description: Large v3 Turbo — fast with near-v3 accuracy

limits.notes: Local whisper.cpp transcription runs on the local machine rather than a hosted API, so provider-side upload and duration caps do not apply.

gcloud.description: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text v2 via gcloud CLI auth and direct REST requests

chirp_3.description: Chirp 3 synchronous transcription with diarization, automatic punctuation, and word timing

limits.notes: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text V2 synchronous Recognize requests are limited to 10 MB or 60 seconds for inline local audio. AutoShow uses the us-speech.googleapis.com regional endpoint with location us and relies on the existing split-and-merge path for larger inputs.

aws.description: Amazon Transcribe batch speech-to-text via AWS CLI

standard.description: Standard batch transcription with language identification and speaker labels

limits.notes: Amazon Transcribe batch jobs require S3 input in the same AWS Region as the transcription request. Current AWS quotas document a 2 GB maximum audio file size and a 14,400-second maximum audio file length for standard transcription.
Deepgram's current docs publish a 2 GB file cap and separately state no limits to audio length for batch transcription, with a 10-minute processing-time cap for Nova/Base/Enhanced requests.

Groq documents a 25 MB free-tier file cap and a 100 MB dev-tier file cap. Direct multipart attachments remain capped at 25 MB, so the current CLI keeps effectiveBytes at 25 MB. No maximum audio duration is currently documented.

Groq documents a 25 MB free-tier file cap and a 100 MB dev-tier file cap. Direct multipart attachments remain capped at 25 MB, so the current CLI keeps effectiveBytes at 25 MB. No maximum audio duration is currently documented.

stt-async-v4.description: Async v4 — diarized asynchronous transcription with token timestamps

limits.notes: Soniox's Files API documents a maximum uploaded file size of 524288000 bytes. Async docs separately cap file duration at 300 minutes. The transcription API also accepts audio_url, but no explicit remote URL size cap is currently published.

standard.description: Batch standard accuracy — faster turnaround with lower cost

limits.notes: Current Speechmatics docs publish a 1 GB limit for multipart uploads and explicitly suggest fetch-by-URL for larger files. They do not publish a current remote URL size cap or a batch-audio duration cap.

enhanced.description: Batch enhanced accuracy — highest accuracy for file transcription

limits.notes: Current Speechmatics docs publish a 1 GB limit for multipart uploads and explicitly suggest fetch-by-URL for larger files. They do not publish a current remote URL size cap or a batch-audio duration cap.

machine.description: Reverb Transcription via Rev async REST API with diarization enabled by default

limits.notes: The 5 TB limit applies to dashboard or source_config submissions; current CLI uploads multipart media files directly.

low_cost.description: Reverb Turbo transcription via Rev async REST API with diarization enabled by default

limits.notes: The 5 TB limit applies to dashboard or source_config submissions; current CLI uploads multipart media files directly.

scribe_v2.description: Scribe v2 — 90+ languages, diarization, word-level timestamps

limits.notes: Standard mode is capped at 10 hours. ElevenLabs also documents a 1-hour cap for multichannel mode, which the current CLI does not use.

Mistral's /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint supports file, file_id, and file_url inputs. Current docs explicitly publish the 3-hour audio-length cap for transcription, but not a dedicated size limit for direct multipart file or external file_url inputs. Mistral's separate /v1/files endpoint documents a 512 MB cap for uploaded files referenced via file_id. AutoShow applies a conservative 78 MiB direct-upload cap based on observed multipart transcription failures around 81 MiB and successful uploads around 80 MiB, so larger files are pre-split instead of failing first.

universal-3-pro.description: Universal-3 Pro — 6 languages, advanced prompting, diarization

limits.notes: AssemblyAI allows larger remote URLs than upload API submissions. The current CLI uploads first, so effectiveBytes tracks the smaller upload cap.

default.description: Default pre-recorded STT — diarization-enabled async transcription

limits.notes: Direct YouTube links are limited to 120 minutes. Current CLI uploads files instead. Enterprise plans can raise the audio-length cap to 4 hours 15 minutes.

subtitle-track.description: Manual or auto-generated English subtitle track via yt-dlp

limits.notes: This adapter reuses subtitle tracks fetched by yt-dlp instead of uploading audio to a hosted STT API, so provider-side file-size and duration request caps do not apply.
